title: Query-wise ranking model by race-lap context, then calibrate
summary: AUC is ranking-based, and pit decisions are relative within local race context.
rationale: AUC is ranking-based, and pit decisions are relative within local race context. CatBoost supports query-wise AUC/ranking objectives, which can learn who is most likely to pit among cars sharing the same race-lap context; this can complement a plain binary classifier.
implementation_hint: Train a second model with group IDs such as `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` or `(Year, Race, LapNumber, dry/wet)` and CatBoost query-wise loss/metric. Blend its OOF predictions with the current binary model, then calibrate the blend on OOF predictions only.
expected_effect: Improves ordering of borderline examples in the same strategic moment, which can help ROC AUC even if raw probabilities are imperfect.
risk: Groups with few positives or tiny size may be noisy; wrong grouping can dilute signal. This is an extension of prior ranking ideas, so gains may be incremental rather than large.
